themes.hook: The $250 Billion Indian IT party just hit a wall. And it’s not because of a recession—it’s because 'Time & Material' billing is dying.
key_data_points: India's IT sector is valued at over $250B
Bajaj Finance already uses 800+ AI agents for Rs 1,600 Cr in disbursals
TCS is partnering with NVIDIA to pivot toward enterprise-scale automation
themes.non_obvious_angle: This is a 'cannibalization' trap. If AI makes a 100-hour coding task take 10 hours, and you bill by the hour, you just lost 90% of your revenue. The traditional linear growth model (more heads = more revenue) is officially broken.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, how do legacy giants like TCS and Infosys pivot their entire sales DNA from 'headcount' to 'value' without crashing their net margins in the short term?

source_articles.title: IT firms rethink billing as artificial intelligence reshapes work
Indian companies outpacing incumbents via AI adoption
TCS Launches AI Platform with NVIDIA

themes.hook: Indian banks are facing a 'silent' liquidity crisis. The Credit-to-Deposit (CD) ratio is at a staggering 82.4%—and the money isn't coming back to savings accounts.
key_data_points: UPI accounts for 81% of retail digital payments in FY25
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Equity Cult' isn't just a bull market phenomenon; it's a structural drain on the banking system. When households move money from FDs to Mutual Funds/Zerodha, banks lose the low-cost fuel they need to fund India's infrastructure boom.
themes.your_take_prompt: Are we entering an era where banks must stop being 'vaults' and start being 'investment platforms' just to keep deposits from fleeing to the markets?

source_articles.title: India’s bank credit surges 14.5% YoY; CD ratio stays elevated
Veteran Indian Banker Says New Tools Needed as Deposits Fizzle
UPI dominated retail digital payments with 81 pc share

themes.hook: Nasdaq was once the 'promised land' for Indian tech. Now? They’re all coming home. MakeMyTrip is eyeing an Indian IPO, and Jio is lining up banks for the big one.
key_data_points: MakeMyTrip has been listed on Nasdaq since 2010 (15 years)
Reliance is lining up banks for a Jio IPO that could re-rate the entire digital economy
US exports from India dropped 13% in February
themes.non_obvious_angle: This isn't just about 'Ghar Wapsi.' It’s about valuation discovery. Indian investors now pay a 'scarcity premium' for local tech leaders that US investors treat as just another risky emerging market ticker.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does the 'reverse flip' signal that India’s public markets have finally matured enough to handle 'growth-at-all-costs' tech valuations better than Wall Street?

source_articles.title: MakeMyTrip eyes India IPO for growth
Reliance lines up banks for Jio IPO
Makemytrip reviving plans for an India listing

themes.hook: We’re celebrating India’s 'educated' workforce, but the data tells a darker story. Only 23% of Indians have a salary—and our new curriculum rewards Yoga over Python.
key_data_points: Salaried workers make up only 23% of India's workforce
New college credit systems inadvertently incentivize 'easy' skills over technical rigor
100,000 new jobs are likely in the Data Centre boom, but they require specialized engineering
themes.non_obvious_angle: We are optimizing for 'degree completion' rather than 'employability.' By allowing students to game the credit system with easier subjects, we are diluting the quality of the very talent pool meant to drive 'Viksit Bharat.'
themes.your_take_prompt: In an era of Agentic AI, is India’s higher education policy moving in the exact opposite direction of where the global labor market is headed?

source_articles.title: Yoga over Python: how India’s new college curriculum rewards the easiest skills
Salaried workers are a minority in India's workforce (23%)
Data centre boom brightens engineering job prospects

themes.hook: Think the West Asia crisis is just about oil prices? Tell that to the Alphonso mango exporter. The 'hidden' cost of shipping is the real inflation killer.
key_data_points: India is paying ~$99/barrel for Russian crude including shipping costs
Middle East disruptions could impact City Gas Distribution (CGD) volumes by 8-10%
War surcharges on cargo are surging for Indian exporters
themes.non_obvious_angle: It’s not just the fuel cost; it's the 'Slow Steaming' economics. When ships move slower to save fuel or avoid zones, it effectively acts as a massive tax on Indian working capital—money is locked in inventory for 20-30% longer.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is India's reliance on the Strait of Hormuz the single biggest structural risk to our 'Make in India' export ambitions?

source_articles.title: From Aluminium To Alphonso Mangoes: West Asia Tensions Paralysing Exports
India is paying ~$99 a barrel for Russian crude Urals
